references.text: Backer, CA & Bakhuizen van den Brink. 1965. Flora of Java 2: 437-441.
Geesink, R. 1973. A synopsis of the genus Swertia (Gent.) in Malesia. Blumea 21: 179-183

Leenhouts, PW. 1962. Loganiaceae. Flora Malesiana, Series 1 6: 293-387 (includes Fagraea)

Mansibang, JA, LJC Alterado, AGB Espinosa & EM Blancaflor. 2025. Notes on the poorly known Exacum loheri (H. Hara) Klack. (Gentianaceae) and a key to the Philippine species of Exacum. Philippine Journal of Science 154: 1227-1230.

Mansion, G. 2004. A new classification of the polyphyletic genus Centaurium Hill (Chironiinae, Gentianaceae): Description of the New World endenic Zeltnera, and reinstatement of Gyrandra Griseb. and Schenkia Griseb. Taxon 53: 719-740
Murata, J. 1989. A synopsis of Tripterospermum (Gentianaceae). Journal of the Faculty of Science, Univ. Tokyo Sec. III, 16 (4): 273-339.
Royen, P van. 1964. Nova Guinea Bot. 17: 369-416.
Sugumaran, M & KM Wong. 2012. Studies in Malesian Gentianaceae I: Fagraea sensu lato - complex genus or several genera? A molecular phylogenetic study. Gardens' Bulletin Singapore 64: 301-332
Sugumaran, M & KM Wong. 2014. Studies in Malesian Gentianaceae VI: A revision of Utania in the Malay Pensinsula with two new species. Plant Ecology and Evolution 147: 213-223
Thiv, M. 2003. A taxonomic revision of Canscora, Cracosna, Duplipetala, Hoppea, Microrphium, Phylloccyclus and Schinziella (Gentianaceae-Canscorinae). Blumea 48: 1-46
Wong, KM & JB Sugau. 1996. A revision of Fagraea (Loganiaceae) in Borneo, with notes on related Malesian species and 21 new species. Sandakania 8: 1-93.
Wong, KM & M Sugumaran. 2012. Studies in Malesian Gentianaceae II: A taxonomic framework for the Fagraea complex, including the new genus Limahlania. Gardens' Bulletin Singapore 64: 481-495
Wong, KM & M Sugumaran. 2012. Studies in Malesian Gentianaceae III: Cyrtophyllum reapplied to the Fagraea fragrans alliance. Gardens' Bulletin Singapore 64: 497-510
Wong, KM & M Sugumaran. 2016. Studies in Malesian Gentianaceae, VII. A revision of Fagraea in the Malay Peninsula with five new species. Sandakania 21: 65-130.
Wong, KM, M Sugumaran & JB Sugau. 2013. Studies in Malesian Gentianaceae V: The Fagraea complex in Borneo: New generic assignments and recombinations. Gardens' Bulletin Singapore 65; 235-239.

species.citation: (Vahl) R.Br. ex Roem. & Schult. , Syst. Veg., 15th Ed. 3 (1818) 301 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 367; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 318; Thiv, Blumea 48 (2003) 11; ≡ Gentiana diffusa Vahl , Symb. Bot. (Vahl) 3 (1794) 47.
species.distribution: Africa, Asia, Australia, Malesia. LUZON: Abra (photos), Apayao, Bataan, Benguet, Bulacan, Cagayan (photos), Cavite, Ilocos Norte, Mountain Province, NCR, Nueva Vizcaya, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Zambales, MINDORO, PANAY.

species.citation: Miq. , Fl. Ned. Ind. 2 (1857) 558 ; Thiv, Blumea 48 (2003) 15.
species.distribution: Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Philippines. MINDANAO: Zamboanga del Norte, PANAY (photos).
species.notes: Moist open places in grasslands, fields; heavy soil. Fl. Jan-Aug. 0-600m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 15 September 2024

species.citation: (Roxb.) DC. , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 9 (1845) 31; ≡ Fagraea fragrans Roxb. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 2 (1824) 32 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 314; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 304; Wong & Sugau, Tree Fl. Sabah & Sarawak 2 (1996) 206. = ?Fagraea cochinchinensis (Lour.) A.Chev. , Jard. Bot. Saigon (1919) 65 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 314.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Borneo, India, Java Sea, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra, Thailand, Vietnam. BALABAC, BUSUANGA, CULION, MINDORO, PALAWAN.
species.notes: Lowland forests, especially secondary and disturbed ones. 0-800m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 19 October 2025

species.citation: (H.Hara) Klack. , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 126 (2006) 478 ; Mansibang et al., PJS 154 (2025) 1227; ≡ Cotylanthera loheri Hara , J. Jap. Bot. 50 (1975) 327; Type: Loher 5189 (K, holo), Luzon: Rizal, Montalban, 26-Oct-1895.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Rizal.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 7c (1912) 96 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 317; Type: Vanoverbergh 758.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Mountain Province.

species.citation: (Blume) Klack. , Bot. Jahrb. Syst. 126 (2006) 478; ≡ Cotylanthera tenuis Blume , Bijdr. fl. Ned. Ind. (1826) 707 ; Merrill, PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 223; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 317; Type: from Java. = Eophylon tenellum A.Gray , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 11 (1869) 23.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Philippines. CEBU (photos), LUZON: Bataan, Laguna, Mountain Province, Rizal, Sorsogon, MANGSEE, MINDANAO, MINDORO (photos), NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY.
species.notes: Low and medium elevation damp primary forests. (?-)1700m.

species.citation: Roxb. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 1 (1820) 413. = Exacum chironioides Griseb. , Gen. Sp. Gent. (1839) 109 ; Fern.-Vill., Novis. App. (1880) 136; Merr., Sp. Blancoanae (1918) 307; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 317.
species.distribution: Malesia. CULION, LUZON: Abra (photos), Albay, Cagayan, Ilocos Sur, NCR, Nueva Vizcaya, Pangasinan, Rizal, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao, Davao del Sur, Zamboanga del Norte (photos), MINDORO, PANAY.
species.notes: Low and medium elevation wet grasslands, swamps, etc. 335-800m (photos).

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 8 November 2024

species.citation: Jack , Malayan Misc. 2 (1822) 82 ; Elmer, LPB 2 (1909) 509; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 314; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 326; Coritico et al., J. Trop. Biol. & Cons. 19 (2022) 149. = Fagraea epiphytica Elmer , LPB 8 (1915) 2743. ssp. auriculata. Distribution: Borneo, Indochina, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand. LEYTE, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, PANAY, SAMAR. Native. ssp. parviflora Leenh., Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 6 (1962) 328. = Fagraea curranii Merr., PJS 13c (1918) 50; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 314. Distribution: Moluccas, Philippines. LUZON: Laguna, Quezon, MINDORO. Native.
species.distribution: CEBU (photos), LEYTE, LUZON: Apayao (photos), Isabela (photos), Laguna, Quezon, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Davao del Sur, Surigao del Sur (photos), MINDORO, NEGROS (photos), PALAWAN, PANAY, SAMAR.

species.citation: G.Don , Gen. Hist. 4 (1837) 69, as 'blumii' ; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 320; Wong & Sugau, Tree Fl. Sabah & Sarawak 2 (1996) 202. = Fagraea cuernosensis Elmer , LPB 2 (1909) 600 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 314. = Fagraea negrosensis Elmer , LPB 2 (1909) 599 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 315. ≠ Fagraea obovata auct. non Wall. ; Miq., Fl. Ind. Bat. 2 (1857) 369; Merr., EPFP 3 (1923) 315, pro parte. ssp. blumei. Distribution: Borneo, Java, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra. NEGROS. Notes: (500-)1200-1800(-2200)m. Native. ssp. plumeriiflora (DC.) Leenh., Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 6 (1962) 323; ≡ Fagraea plumeriiflora DC., Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 9 (1845) 29; Elmer, LPB 2 (1909) 598; LPB 3 (1910) 857; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 315, excl. specimens from Sulawesi. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CATANDUANES, LEYTE, LUZON, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Davao del Sur, Surigao del Sur, PANAY, SAMAR. Notes: Intermediate forms between ssp. plumeriiflora and ssp. blumei occur in Borneo, Negros and Mindanao. 500-2000m. Native.
species.distribution: CAMIGUIN, CATANDUANES, LEYTE, LUZON: Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao Oriental (photos), Davao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, Misamis Oriental (photos), Surigao del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS, PANAY, SAMAR.

species.citation: Scheff. , Flora 32 (1869) 309 ; Wong & Sugau, Tree Fl. Sabah & Sarawak 2 (1996) 202; ≡ Fagraea auriculata ssp. borneensis (Scheff.) Leenh. , Fl. Males., 1st Ser. 6 (1962) 328; Type: from Sarawak. = Fagraea nonok Elmer , LPB 3 (1910) 858 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 315. = Fagraea jackii Elmer , LPB 8 (1915) 2744.
species.distribution: Borneo, Lesser Sunda Isls, Philippines. MINDORO, PALAWAN, SIBUYAN.

species.citation: Thunb. , Kongl. Vetensk. Acad. Nya Handl. 3 (1782) 132 ; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 315. = Fagraea obovata Wall. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 2 (1824) 33 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 315, pro parte. = Fagraea loheri Merr. , PJS 27 (1925) 49 ; Merrill, EPFP 4 (1926) 254; Type: Loher.
species.distribution: China, Hong Kong, India, Indochina, Myanmar, Solomon Isls, Sri Lanka, Taiwan, Thailand. BATAN, BILIRAN, CAMIGUIN, CATANDUANES, LAGEN, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Apayao (photos), Aurora (photos), Bataan, Benguet, Cagayan (photos), Camarines Sur, Ifugao, Isabela, Laguna, Mountain Province, Pampanga, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Davao, Misamis Oriental (photos), Surigao del Norte, Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, ROMBLON, SAMAR, SIBUYAN, SULU ARCHIPELAGO, TAWI-TAWI.
species.notes: Throughout Malesia, throughout the Philippines; high-climbing woody hemi-epiphyte in forests. 0-2500m.

species.citation: Elmer , LPB 3 (1910) 859 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 315; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 334; Fernando et al., Forest Flora of Dinagat (2017) 40.
species.distribution: Moluccas, Philippines. DINAGAT, SIBUYAN.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 3c (1908) 260 ; Elmer, LPB 2 (1909) 597; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 315. = Fagraea macgregorii Merr. , PJS 13c (1918) 51.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. CATANDUANES, LUZON: Apayao, Aurora, Camarines Sur, Ifugao, Isabela, Laguna, Nueva Vizcaya (photos), Quezon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 19 April 2026

species.citation: Hook.f. , Hooker's Icon. Pl. 15 (1883) t. 1440 B. ssp. apoensis (Merr.) Halda, Acta Mus. Richnov., Sect. Nat. 3 (1995) 45. = Gentiana apoensis Merr., PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 223; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 318; Type: Copeland 1161 & 1432 (PNH, ?lost, syn), Mindanao: Davao, Mt Apo, summit area, c. 3000m, occasional in turf, but inconspicuous, not common, Apr/Oct-1904; de Vore & Hoover 371 (PNH, ?lost, syn), Mindanao: Davao, Mt Apo, May-1903. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Benguet, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur. Notes: 2865-3000m. Native.
species.distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines. LUZON: Benguet, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur.

species.citation: Merr. , PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 224 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 319; Type: Merrill 4443 (PNH, holo, ?lost), Luzon: Benguet, Kabayan. On damp, rocky banks, in open lands, rare, c. 1300m, 27-Oct-1905.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Benguet.

species.citation: Halda , Acta Mus. Richnov., Sect. Nat. 3 (1995) 46; ≡ Gentiana copelandii Elmer , LPB 7 (1915) 2671, nom. illeg., non Greene ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 319; Type: Elmer 11692.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Davao, Davao del Sur.

species.citation: Hayata , J. Coll. Sci. Imp. Univ. Tokyo 25 (1908) 168. ssp. luzoniensis (Merr.) Halda, Acta Mus. Richnov., Sect. Nat. 3 (1995) 39. = Gentiana luzoniensis Merr., PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 224; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 319; Type: Merrill 4558 (NY, US, iso), Luzon: Mountain Province, Mt Data, on dry, open grassy slopes in thin pine forests at c. 2100m, 4-Nov-1905. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Benguet, Mountain Province, NEGROS. Notes: 2000-3000m. Native.
species.distribution: Philippines, Taiwan. LUZON: Benguet, Mountain Province, NEGROS.

species.citation: Willd. , Neue Schriften Ges. Naturf. Freunde Berlin 3 (1801) 434 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 318; Thiv, Blumea 48 (2003) 28.
species.distribution: India. LUZON: Abra, Benguet, Ilocos Norte, Pangasinan.
species.notes: Thiv argues that outside of India, this plant is non indigenous in occurrence. In the Philippines reported from open places. Rare or local. 300-1800m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 24 May 2018

species.citation: C.B.Clarke , J. Asiat. Soc. Bengal, Pt. 2, Nat. Hist. 74 (1906) 88 ; Thiv, Blumea 48 (2003) 31. ssp. elmerianum (Regalado & Soejarto) Thiv, Blumea 48 (2003) 33; ≡ Microrphium elmerianum Regalado & Soejarto, Novon 7 (1997) 77; Type: Soejarto et al. 6499 (F, holo; A, PNH, iso), Palawan: Puerto Princesa, Bacungan Hills, slopes above Nagtabon beach, 9°51’N, 118°37’E. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. PALAWAN. Notes: Fl. Feb-Aug. 50-100m. Native.
species.distribution: Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Thailand. MINDORO, PALAWAN.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 30 October 2019

species.citation: (L.) G.Mans. , Taxon 53 (2004) 726; ≡ Centaurium spicatum (L.) Fritsch , Mitt. Naturwiss. Vereins Univ. Wien 5 (1907) 97; ≡ Erythraea spicata (L.) Pers. , Syn. Pl. (Pers.) 1 (1905) 283 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 317; ≡ Gentiana spicata L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 230.
species.distribution: Europe. BATAN.
species.notes: Introduced into Taiwan by early Portuguese colonists, extending thereafter southward to the Batanes Isls Group. Open places at low elevation.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 28 October 2025

species.citation: C.B.Rob. , PJS 3c (1909) 214 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 319; Antipuesto et al., J. Wildlife Biodiv. 9 (2025) 508; Type: R.S. Williams 1529.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Benguet, Ifugao, Ilocos Sur, Mountain Province.
species.notes: Open slopes, extending into the mossy forest. 1200-3000m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 1 August 2017

species.citation: (S.Vidal) J.Murata , J. Fac. Sci. Univ. Tokyo, Sect. 3, Bot. 14 (1989) 305 ; Wang & Chen in Huang, Fl. Taiwan, ed. 2, 4 (1998) 186; ≡ Crawfurdia luzoniensis S.Vidal , Revis. Pl. Vasc. Filip. (1886) 191 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 318; Type: Vidal 1228 (MA), Luzon: Lepanto.
species.distribution: Java, Philippines, Taiwan. LUZON: Benguet, Laguna, Mountain Province, Quezon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato (photos).
species.notes: Mossy forests in very damp shaded ravines. 1100-2400m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 7 July 2026

species.citation: (Blume) K.M.Wong, Sugumaran & Sugau , Gard. Bull. Singapore 65 (2013) 237; ≡ Fagraea cuspidata Blume , Mus. Bot. 1 (1850) 170 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 314; Wong & Sugau, Tree Fl. Sabah & Sarawak 2 (1996) 204.
species.distribution: Borneo, Philippines. BALABAC.
species.notes: Mixed dipterocarp forests, in gaps and clearings, also secondary forests and forest edges. (?-)1500m.

species.citation: (K.M.Wong & Sugau) K.M.Wong, Sugumaran & Sugau , Gard. Bull. Singapore 65 (2013) 237 ; Fernando et al., Forest Flora of Dinagat (2017) 104; ≡ Fagraea philippinensis K.M.Wong & Sugau , Sandakania 8 (1996) 35; Type: FB 3270 Ahern’s Collector (K, holo). ≠ Fagraea racemosa auct. non Jack ex Wall. ; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 311, pro parte.
species.distribution: Borneo, Philippines. BALABAC, BASILAN, BILIRAN, BOHOL, CEBU, DINAGAT, LEYTE, LUZON: Quezon, Rizal, MANICANI, MINDANAO: Agusan del Sur, Misamis Occidental, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN, PANAY, POLILLO, SAMAR, SIBUYAN.

species.citation: (Baker) K.M.Wong, Sugumaran & Sugau , Gard. Bull. Singapore 65 (2013) 238 ; Malabrigo et al., Flora of Mt Mantalingahan Protected Landscape (2023) 120; ≡ Fagraea spicata Baker , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1896 (1896) 25 ; Wong & Sugau, Tree Fl. Sabah & Sarawak 2 (1996) 214. = Fagraea congestiflora Elmer , LPB 8 (1915) 2741 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 316. ≠ Fagraea racemosa auct. non Jack ex Wall. ; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 311.
species.distribution: Borneo, Philippines. PALAWAN.
species.notes: Lowland to montane forests, frequently in gaps and also secondary forests. (?-)60m (photos) to 3200m.

species.citation: (Wall.) Sugumaran , Gard. Bull. Singapore 64 (2012) 493 ; Cortez et al., Check List 19 (2023) 988; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific biodiv. 17 (2024) 11; ≡ Fagraea volubilis Wall. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 2 (1824) 36 ; Wong & Sugau, Tree Fl. Sabah & Sarawak 2 (1996) 215. = Fagraea scholaris Blanco , Fl. Filip., 2nd Ed. (1845) 93 ; Fl. Filip. ed. 3, 1 (1877) 171. ≠ Fagraea racemosa auct. non Jack ex Wall. ; Merr., EPFP 3 (1923) 315; Leenh., Fl. Males. ser. 1, 6 (1962) 311.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Borneo, Java, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. BALABAC, CATANDUANES (photos), CEBU (photos), DINAGAT (photos), HOMONHON, LUZON: Aurora (photos), Rizal, Sorsogon (photos), MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Davao Oriental (photos), Misamis Oriental (photos), Surigao del Norte (photos), Zamboanga del Norte (photos), MINDORO, NEGROS (photos), PALAWAN, PANAY, SIARGAO, SIBUYAN, TABLAS.
species.notes: Lowland forests, including secondary forests and forest edges. 0-1100m.
